jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer presumably crashes or has some mishap (car accident implied by tire screeching)
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation followed by pain
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfectly integrated character moment with maximum economy

jokes.text: Fire! Fire! Fire! [Gasps] Oh, no! What do I save? [Screeches] Trophies, trophies. Oh- Ooh! Attendance.
jokes.setup: Fire alarm goes off, character panics about what to save
jokes.punchline: Priorities escalate from trophies to just attendance records
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from valuable items to mundane bureaucracy shows character priorities

jokes.text: Good work, everyone. We're sure to be first in line for Duff Days.
jokes.setup: Character acts like they accomplished something important with the fire drill
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was all just to get to a beer festival early
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - takes serious fire drill preparation and reveals selfish motivation

jokes.text: [Chuckles] I know. I'm a character.
jokes.setup: Someone calls out the character for setting off smoke alarm for beer festival
jokes.punchline: Character self-consciously acknowledges their quirky behavior
jokes.explanation: Light meta humor - character awareness of being a 'character' type

jokes.text: Rockin' Fun Zone? That sounds fun.
jokes.setup: Homer hears about designated drivers' area
jokes.punchline: His literal, simple interpretation of obviously sarcastic name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his innocent obliviousness to obvious sarcasm

jokes.text: I don't see the need for razor wire.
jokes.setup: Marge observes the designated drivers' area
jokes.punchline: Understated reaction to what's obviously a prison-like setup
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag setup with Marge's characteristic understated concern

jokes.text: Early to bed. [Hiccups] Early to rise. Ya think-Ya think you're better than me? Huh?
jokes.setup: Ben Franklin animatronic starts with his famous saying
jokes.punchline: Devolves into drunken confrontational behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion - noble historical figure corrupted into belligerent drunk

jokes.text: Now, fresh from his appearance before the House Subcommittee on Teenage Alcoholism... Duff Man!
jokes.setup: Announcing Duff Man's entrance
jokes.punchline: He's 'fresh from' testifying about teen alcoholism while promoting beer
jokes.explanation: Sharp corporate satire - highlighting the hypocrisy of alcohol marketing

jokes.text: And now, the local lug who fills your mug with the drug you chug- oh,yeah- give it up for Moe Szyslak.
jokes.setup: Flowery introduction building up to local bartender
jokes.punchline: Elaborate rhyming that ends with just 'Moe Szyslak' - bathetic deflation
jokes.explanation: Good buildup and deflation - elaborate intro for unglamorous local character

jokes.text: That spells 'Duff.' Go, Moe! Boo, everyone else.
jokes.setup: Trick pouring demonstration
jokes.punchline: Blunt, unsporting cheering for friend
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character moment showing loyal but unsportsmanlike support

jokes.text: Goat. Uh, close enough!
jokes.setup: Trivia question about what Duff beer is brewed from
jokes.punchline: Wrong answer 'goat' is accepted as 'close enough'
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist humor - wrong answer acceptance suggests low standards

jokes.text: You can really taste the goat.
jokes.setup: Following up on previous 'goat' answer
jokes.punchline: Deadpan confirmation that adds to the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the absurdity with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Moo? Lenny, you were supposed to be 'E.' See what happens when you skip rehearsal.
jokes.setup: Group trying to spell out 'MOE' with their bodies
jokes.punchline: Lenny messes up by saying 'Moo' instead of being letter 'E'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lenny's confusion and the group's amateur preparation

jokes.text: You said if I slept with you, I wouldn't have to touch the drunk. Duff Man says a lot of things. Oh, yeah!
jokes.setup: Titania complains about having to handle drunk contestant
jokes.punchline: Duff Man casually dismisses previous sexual promises with his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about sexual exploitation delivered with Duff Man's oblivious enthusiasm

jokes.text: That's a mug you don't wanna chug.
jokes.setup: Photographer sees Moe's face for calendar photo
jokes.punchline: Rhyming put-down about his ugliness using beer terminology
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay connecting beer slang to insult about appearance

jokes.text: Knock it off, Larry. We can't put this face on our calendar.
jokes.setup: Photographer taking pictures of contest winner Moe
jokes.punchline: Blunt rejection of his face for the calendar
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe humor - direct insult creates uncomfortable situation for Moe

jokes.text: When I get home, there are gonna be a lot of open pickle jars.
jokes.setup: Moe angry and frustrated after being rejected
jokes.punchline: Channels anger into domestic task rather than violence
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up violent threat but deflates to mundane household chore

jokes.text: Then I'd like a rattle and a 'wollypop.' Actually, I would like a 'wollypop.'
jokes.setup: Lisa sarcastically dismisses balloon as childish
jokes.punchline: Gets caught up in cute mispronunciation and actually wants one
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's intellectual facade cracking at cute wordplay

jokes.text: Those balloons won't biodegrade for 10,000 years. If Bart gets a wollypop, I want a wollypop.
jokes.setup: Lisa gives environmental lecture about balloons
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons principles for sibling rivalry
jokes.explanation: Good character work - Lisa's high-minded principles immediately undermined by childish wants

jokes.text: Hey, Moe, this license expired in 1973... and it's only good in Rhode Island, and it's signed by you. Yeah, yeah, I've been meaning to get that updated... for this state and real.
jokes.setup: Homer notices problems with Moe's liquor license
jokes.punchline: Moe casually admits to completely fraudulent license
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Moe's corner-cutting - from expired to wrong state to completely fake

jokes.text: Hey. 'Viva la'- 'Kiss Me'- What? Hey! Ah, for the love of Jeff.
jokes.setup: Moe tries to read stickers covering his face on calendar
jokes.punchline: Gets increasingly frustrated, ends with unusual exclamation 'for the love of Jeff'
jokes.explanation: Character-specific frustration with odd religious-style exclamation using 'Jeff' instead of 'God'

jokes.text: Moe, it's all relative. Is Lenny really that dumb? Huh? Is Barney that drunk? [Moans] Is Homer that lazy, bald and fat? [Sighs] Oh, my God! It's worse than I thought!
jokes.setup: Carl tries to console Moe by suggesting everyone has flaws
jokes.punchline: Each example confirms the stereotypes, making Homer despair
jokes.explanation: Great structure - intended comfort becomes devastating confirmation of everyone's worst traits

jokes.text: Like, you ever been to White Castle? Oh, boy. Pigtown, U.S.A.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to console Moe about ugly people
jokes.punchline: Uses White Castle as example, calls it 'Pigtown, U.S.A.'
jokes.explanation: Homer's blunt insult masked as helpful comparison - typical insensitive comfort attempt

jokes.text: Come on. Look at me. I'm a gargoyle... what, with the cauliflower ear there and the lizard lips- Little rat eyes. Caveman brow. Don't forget that fish snout.
jokes.setup: Moe cataloguing his physical flaws
jokes.punchline: Friends helpfully add more insults instead of consoling
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - friends make situation worse by enthusiastically adding to his self-deprecation

jokes.text: Or listen to. Or be with.
jokes.setup: Following up on Moe's physical appearance complaints
jokes.punchline: Friends expand criticism beyond looks to personality
jokes.explanation: Efficient escalation - expands the insults from physical to complete personality

jokes.text: That's why they won't let Bill Maher on TV before midnight.
jokes.setup: Homer discussing emphasis on looks in society
jokes.punchline: Uses Bill Maher as example of TV appearance scheduling
jokes.explanation: Topical humor about TV personality - implies Maher is too ugly for primetime

jokes.text: Maybe they could dynamite Mount Crapmore here and carve me a new kisser. But I'd look good on the outside, right? Yeah, but you'd feel bad inside. Plastic surgery it is!
jokes.setup: Moe considers plastic surgery, Marge warns about feeling bad inside
jokes.punchline: Moe completely ignores the emotional warning, focuses only on looking good
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Moe's complete shallow focus ignoring deeper wisdom from Marge

jokes.text: But I'd look good on the outside, right? Yeah, but you'd feel bad inside. Plastic surgery it is!
jokes.setup: Homer warns about feeling bad inside despite looking good outside
jokes.punchline: Moe only cares about the external appearance benefit
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moe's shallow priorities despite attempted wisdom

jokes.text: I wish I could be entertained by two cents worth of rubber shaped like some colorful animal... dancing and twirling, dancing and twirling.
jokes.setup: Lisa watches Maggie play with balloon
jokes.punchline: Gets mesmerized despite claiming to be above such simple entertainment
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa moment - intellectual pretension undermined by genuine childlike fascination

jokes.text: I hope they're hoverbikes. Oh, man, that would be so awesome!
jokes.setup: Homer says he'll get bikes to chase balloon
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately imagines futuristic hoverbikes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - turns mundane situation into sci-fi fantasy

jokes.text: First, we must install buttocks. Nah, nah, nah, no luxury items. Just a face.
jokes.setup: Plastic surgeon discussing what Moe needs
jokes.punchline: Moe rejects buttocks as too fancy, just wants basic face
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor - treats buttocks as optional luxury item, face as basic necessity

jokes.text: Can the medical mumbo jumbo. Can you fix me or not? Well, we'll see. You know, most faces need a little remodeling... but this is a total teardown.
jokes.setup: Moe asks direct question about fixing his face
jokes.punchline: Doctor uses construction metaphor - complete demolition needed
jokes.explanation: Good metaphor comparing face to building construction - emphasizes how ugly Moe is

jokes.text: Oh, stop it. You're worse than Faye Dunaway.
jokes.setup: Moe complaining about pain during procedure
jokes.punchline: Doctor compares him to notoriously difficult actress
jokes.explanation: Hollywood reference humor - comparing patient complaints to difficult celebrity

jokes.text: I'm not riding a girl's bike.
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa chasing balloon on bikes
jokes.punchline: Bart's masculine insecurity about bike gender stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Typical gender stereotype humor, in character for Bart but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'm not riding a girl's bike. Hey, it's getting away! Step on it. Hyah! Hyah! Stop kickin' me! Hyah!
jokes.setup: Kids trying to chase balloon on bikes
jokes.punchline: Bart refuses girl's bike but then desperately tries to catch balloon
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling conflict humor - Bart's pride vs. urgency

jokes.text: Ooh, boy, what a mug! Yeah. You should see his genitals. Would you like to see them? I'm awake here.
jokes.setup: Medical staff commenting on Moe's appearance during surgery
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate discussion of patient's private parts while he's conscious
jokes.explanation: Medical unprofessionalism humor - inappropriate discussion while patient is conscious

jokes.text: Yeah. You should see his genitals. Would you like to see them?
jokes.setup: Doctor commenting on patient's appearance
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate offer to show patient's private parts
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate doctor behavior creates discomfort, somewhat cheap shock humor

jokes.text: Uh-oh. This isn't anesthetic. It's new car smell.
jokes.setup: Medical procedure requiring anesthetic
jokes.punchline: Doctor used car air freshener instead of anesthetic
jokes.explanation: Absurdist medical incompetence with unexpected specific detail

jokes.text: We need a symbol. Something that says we're gay and Republican. A little on the nose, don't you think?
jokes.setup: Gay Republicans discussing their symbol needs
jokes.punchline: One acknowledges their choice is too obvious/cliched
jokes.explanation: Political humor with meta-awareness of being too obvious in their symbolism

jokes.text: 'A gay president in 2084'? We're realistic.
jokes.setup: Homer reads bumper sticker about gay president
jokes.punchline: Character claims 75+ years in future is 'realistic' timeline
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire - absurdly distant future presented as reasonable expectation

jokes.text: What? I'm a monster, right? I knew it. I guess I'll just crawl back to live in a sewer... periodically emerging to sue you.
jokes.setup: Moe thinks his surgery failed when people gasp
jokes.punchline: Plans to live like monster in sewer but still pursue legal action
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - combines dramatic self-pity with practical litigation concerns

jokes.text: Oopsie. We got some leakage. Let me just cauterize that for you. [Searing] [Moe Moaning]
jokes.setup: Doctor notices surgical complication
jokes.punchline: Casual 'oopsie' followed by painful procedure sounds
jokes.explanation: Standard medical malpractice humor - casual attitude toward patient pain

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I died on the operating table, didn't I? Yeah, but just for a minute. It's a funny story. I'll tell you sometime.
jokes.setup: Moe realizes something went wrong during surgery
jokes.punchline: Doctor casually admits patient died but dismisses it as amusing anecdote
jokes.explanation: Dark medical humor - death treated as minor inconvenience and entertainment

jokes.text: A new life. A second chance... for revenge.
jokes.setup: Moe reflects on his transformation giving him new opportunities
jokes.punchline: Instead of positive change, immediately thinks of revenge
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - subverts redemption narrative with Moe's vindictive nature

jokes.text: Hey, Duff Man, let's see how you like a sticker on your face! Yeah! Duff Man... can't breathe. Oh, no! Oh!
jokes.setup: Moe gets revenge by putting sticker on Duff Man's face
jokes.punchline: Duff Man can't breathe through the sticker, escalating to danger
jokes.explanation: Callback revenge that escalates beyond pettiness to actual danger

jokes.text: Hey, Duff Man, let's see how you like a sticker on your face! Yeah! Duff Man... can't breathe. Oh, no! Oh!
jokes.setup: Moe getting revenge on Duff Man for calendar sticker
jokes.punchline: Sticker actually prevents Duff Man from breathing, escalates to dangerous
jokes.explanation: Callback to calendar sticker incident, revenge goes too far as typical Simpsons escalation

jokes.text: Well, I just wanted to show you the face that you could have been kissing. Yeah. Oh, I was just a stupid kid back then. And I feel terrible about hurting you. Will you accept my apology?
jokes.setup: Moe confronts woman who rejected him for revenge
jokes.punchline: Her sincere apology completely derails his revenge plan
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - expected confrontation becomes sincere moment that confuses Moe

jokes.text: Apology? Uh, geez. I wasn't expecting that. Uh- Run!
jokes.setup: Moe is confused by unexpected apology
jokes.punchline: Flees the situation rather than dealing with sincere emotion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - completely unprepared for genuine human kindness

jokes.text: I wanted Mary Ann on Gilligan's Island ugly... not Cornelius on the Planet of the Apes ugly. TV ugly, not ugly-ugly.
jokes.setup: Producer explains why Moe was rejected for TV role
jokes.punchline: Creates hierarchy of ugliness with specific pop culture examples
jokes.explanation: Brilliant pop culture categorization of ugliness levels - highly quotable distinction

jokes.text: I've been called ugly, pug ugly, fugly, pug fugly... but never ugly-ugly.
jokes.setup: Moe catalogs all the ways he's been called ugly
jokes.punchline: The simple 'ugly-ugly' is somehow worse than elaborate variations
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay escalation - shows Moe's familiarity with insults about his appearance

jokes.text: I've been called ugly, pug ugly, fugly, pug fugly... but never ugly-ugly.
jokes.setup: Moe has been called many variations of ugly
jokes.punchline: Catalog of increasingly creative ugly variations, 'ugly-ugly' is new low
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of ugly variations, 'pug fugly' is creative, shows Moe's experience with insults

jokes.text: Remember me? Twenty-five years ago... you said I was too ugly to play Dr. Tad Winslow. I did? Well, that's why pencils have erasers, hon. You're our new Dr. Tad Winslow.
jokes.setup: Moe confronts producer who rejected him years ago
jokes.punchline: Producer immediately hires him, undermining revenge plan
jokes.explanation: Another revenge plan derailed by positive response - pattern of Moe's expectations being wrong

jokes.text: But there can't be two Dr. Tad Winslows! That's going to- Oh. [Moans]
jokes.setup: Previous actor objects to being replaced
jokes.punchline: Gets thrown out window mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Standard slapstick - abrupt violence cutting off dialogue

jokes.text: I don't know if I'll be able to accept Moe as Dr. Tad Winslow. Well, I'm gonna keep watching... as long as they have shocking story twists and endless pillow talk.
jokes.setup: Marge expresses doubts about Moe's casting
jokes.punchline: Homer lists the cheesy soap opera elements he actually wants
jokes.explanation: Character contrast - Marge's concern vs Homer's shallow entertainment priorities

jokes.text: But I love you, Tad, and together we can burn all the cities of the Earth. It's against hospital regulations, damn it!
jokes.setup: Mummy character declares love and apocalyptic plans
jokes.punchline: Doctor's main concern is hospital policy, not global destruction
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - bureaucratic concerns outweigh apocalyptic romance

jokes.text: Eh, it's over. She got a window shade.
jokes.setup: Asked about relationship with girl next door
jokes.punchline: Relationship ends because he can't voyeuristically watch anymore
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and deadpan delivery - reveals creepy basis of 'relationship'

jokes.text: Eh, it's over. She got a window shade.
jokes.setup: Asked about relationship with girl next door
jokes.punchline: Relationship ended by window shade blocking view
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lenny line - implies relationship was just voyeurism, deadpan delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: Hmm. This isn't nearly as complicated as Moe made it seem. [Screaming] [Gurgling]
jokes.setup: Homer confidently tries to pour his own beer
jokes.punchline: Immediate disaster with screaming and gurgling sounds
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff - overconfidence immediately punished

jokes.text: But the one hole I've never been able to fix is the one in my soul!
jokes.setup: Doctor character talks about dedicating life to diseases of head holes
jokes.punchline: Extends medical metaphor to emotional/spiritual emptiness
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay extending medical terminology to metaphorical realm

jokes.text: Hey, I got a gift. As a child, I was bitten by the acting bug. Then it burrowed under my skin and laid eggs in my heart. Now those eggs are hatching, and the feeling is indescribable.
jokes.setup: Moe uses common metaphor about being 'bitten by acting bug'
jokes.punchline: Takes metaphor literally to grotesque parasitic extreme
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extended metaphor - takes cliched expression to disturbing literal extreme

jokes.text: I know what you mean. Our dog had that.
jokes.setup: Following Moe's poetic metaphor about artistic passion
jokes.punchline: Homer treats it as literal veterinary condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - completely literal interpretation deflates Moe's pretension

jokes.text: 'Interior: Coroner's office. Close-up on Dr. Winslow's mangled corpse. Coroner: Let's get that brain out and weigh it and call it a day.' Yep, that does sound kind of bad.
jokes.setup: Homer reads gruesome script description of character's death
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement - 'kind of bad' for graphic death scene
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer understatement - graphic violence described as mildly negative

jokes.text: Well, if they're gonna stomp on my dreams... the least I can do is go out in a blaze of sour grapes.
jokes.setup: Moe planning revenge for being killed off show
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor combining 'blaze of glory' with 'sour grapes'
jokes.explanation: Clever mixed metaphor showing Moe's vengeful but somewhat pathetic attitude

jokes.text: Gabriella's baby shower will be invaded by terrorists... with sexy results. Well, Sister Bernadette will leave the convent and start a softball team... with sexy results.
jokes.setup: Homer revealing future soap opera plot twists
jokes.punchline: Every plot development ends 'with sexy results'
jokes.explanation: Good running gag - absurd soap opera formula where everything leads to sexiness

jokes.text: Gabriella's baby shower will be invaded by terrorists... with sexy results.
jokes.setup: Angel revealing future soap opera plots
jokes.punchline: Terrorist attack somehow has 'sexy results'
jokes.explanation: Perfect soap opera logic - even terrorism becomes sexual, absurd juxtaposition

jokes.text: You idiot. Dr. Winslow was only going to die in a dream. Pink pages always mean a dream. I thought dreams was on goldenrod. No, goldenrod is for coma fantasies.
jokes.setup: Moe ruined show by revealing plots
jokes.punchline: Elaborate color-coding system for different types of unreality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant soap opera bureaucracy - color-coded system for different reality levels

jokes.text: You idiot. Dr. Winslow was only going to die in a dream. Pink pages always mean a dream. I thought dreams was on goldenrod. No, goldenrod is for coma fantasies.
jokes.setup: Producer explains TV script color-coding system
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific categories for different types of unreality
jokes.explanation: Good industry satire - absurdly specific bureaucracy for fictional scenarios

jokes.text: Nah, it's just as well. That handsome face was nice, but it was too much maintenance. I had to wash it, rub it with neat's-foot oil.
jokes.setup: Moe reflects on losing his good looks
jokes.punchline: Treats face like leather goods requiring special oil maintenance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - treats face maintenance like caring for leather boots

jokes.text: And to think... I was about to sell the bar to Hooters. Yeah, you were- D'oh!
jokes.setup: Moe mentions almost selling bar to Hooters
jokes.punchline: Homer's excited reaction reveals this would have upset him greatly
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's priorities clearly revealed by reaction

jokes.text: When my face was crushed, why did it go back to my old face? Shouldn't it have turned into some kind of third face that was different? Don't make no-
jokes.setup: Moe questions the logic of his transformation
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off before completing logical objection to cartoon physics
jokes.explanation: Meta humor - character questioning cartoon logic before being shut down
